Functions: handleVisibilityChange()
Reason for Update: Detects when browser tab is hidden using HTML5 Page Visibility API (document.hidden). Replaced basic window.onblur.

Functions: handleFullscreenChange()
Reason for Update: Logs a fullscreen_exit event when student leaves enforced fullscreen mode.

Functions: removeMonitoring
Reason for Update: Tears down proctoring: clears webcam interval reference and removes all global event listeners on session exit.

Functions: setupMonitoring()
Reason for Update: Registers both Page Visibility and window blur/focus listeners in a single setup call.

Functions: startWebcam()
Reason for Update: Starts camera via getUserMedia with constrained resolution (320x240) to limit memory usage.

Functions: captureAndUploadScreenshot()
Reason for Update: Captures current webcam frame as a compressed Base64 JPEG and sends it to the server for proctoring evidence.

Functions: captureSegment()
Update Made: Records 5-second rolling video buffer using MediaRecorder API. Segments are stored as Blobs on the client and overwritten unless an anomaly is detected — no footage is transmitted to the server.
Reason for Update: Atharv Dhuvad

Functions: pre(save) hook — recalculates summary.totalEvents + highSeverityEvents + totalTabSwitches + totalFullscreenExits
Reason for Update: Mongoose pre-save hook automatically recalculates all violation summary counts on every document save.

Functions: POST /submissions — auto-flags submission when tabSwitchCount > 5 or fullscreenExitCount > 3
Reason for Update: Automatically flags submissions that exceed violation thresholds so teachers do not need to flag manually.

Functions: blockRestrictedInputs() — prevents & logs copy / paste / cut events
Reason for Update: Intercepts clipboard events to prevent data exfiltration during the exam.

Functions: handleContextMenu() — prevents right-click context menu
Reason for Update: Disables the browser right-click menu to block Inspect Element access.

Functions: handleKeyDown() — blocks F12 and Ctrl+Shift+I; logs dev_tools_opened event
Reason for Update: Blocks developer tool shortcuts and logs every attempt as a proctoring event.

Functions: POST /proctoring/face-detection — if facesDetected === 0: pushes face_not_detected event
Reason for Update: Deducts 10 trust score points and logs face_not_detected when the student leaves the camera frame.

Functions: POST /proctoring/face-detection — if facesDetected > 1: pushes multiple_faces event
Reason for Update: Applies a 15-point trust score penalty and logs a multiple_faces event to flag possible collaboration.

Functions: faceDetectionResults — array of {timestamp / facesDetected / confidence / screenshot}
Reason for Update: Stores a timestamped visual evidence log for every face detection event for post-exam auditing.

Functions: POST to /proctoring/face-detection via logFaceDetection()
Reason for Update: Client-side call that sends real-time face count and screenshot to the backend for trust score calculation.

Functions: startWebcam() — getUserMedia() + floating <video> element
Reason for Update: Initialises camera and renders a persistent floating video preview so the proctor can see the student's feed.

Functions: Periodic screenshot timer (every 60 s)
Reason for Update: Schema supports scheduled snapshot capture triggered every 60 seconds to ensure continuous coverage.

Functions: handleBlur() — window.addEventListener(blur + handleBlur)
Reason for Update: Registers a global blur listener that flags a focus-loss violation whenever the browser window loses focus.

Functions: fetchDashboardData() — parallel Promise.all() of getDashboardStats() and getActiveSessions()
Reason for Update: Replaced sequential API calls with parallel fetching to reduce dashboard load time.

Functions: fetchActiveSessions() — setInterval every 30 000 ms
Reason for Update: Polls the active sessions list every 30 seconds so teachers see live violation data without refreshing.

Functions: GET /proctoring/active — returns sessions where status = active
Reason for Update: New endpoint returning only active sessions with populated student and exam fields.

Functions: GET /proctoring/flagged — returns sessions where status = flagged or reviewStatus = pending
Reason for Update: New endpoint providing a dedicated view of sessions that need teacher review.

Functions: GET /reports/stats/dashboard — role-aware aggregated statistics
Reason for Update: Returns computed counts (active/flagged sessions
Testing - Ticket_ids raised: today's events) in one API call.

Functions: handleViewSession(session) — maps event log into chronological review interface
Reason for Update: Loads and displays full session telemetry with type badge + severity + timestamp + details per event.

Functions: Screenshots gallery — renders session.screenshots[] with thumbnails + timestamps
Reason for Update: Displays a timestamped grid of captured screenshots so reviewers can verify flagged infractions visually.

Functions: handleReviewSession(status) — updates session to cleared / reviewed / flagged
Reason for Update: Allows the reviewer to finalise a session decision and attach notes before saving.

Functions: GET /proctoring/:sessionId — returns full session telemetry
Reason for Update: Aggregates events

Functions: PUT /proctoring/:sessionId/review — persists reviewer identity + status + notes
Reason for Update: Saves the reviewer's name
Testing - Ticket_ids raised: and professional notes as an audit record in the session document.

Functions: POST /proctoring/event — broadcasts proctoring_alert when trustScore < 50
Reason for Update: Automatically sends a real-time alert notification to proctoring staff when a session's trust score drops below 50%.

Functions: type enum: mcq, short_answer, fill_blank, true_false, essay
Reason for Update: Polymorphic question schema supporting five question formats. Word limits and difficulty levels still pending.

Functions: question: String (required) / options: [String] / correctAnswer: String (required) / points: Number (default:1) / explanation: String / order: Number
Reason for Update: Initial MongoDB schema for exam questions with all required and optional fields.

Functions: settings: shuffleQuestions / shuffleOptions / showResultsImmediately / allowBackNavigation / requireWebcam / requireFullscreen / maxAttempts / passingScore / autoSubmitOnTimeUp
Reason for Update: Exam configuration flags for controlling proctoring requirements and student experience during an exam.

Functions: POST /exams — creates exam with title, description, questions[], scheduledAt, duration, endTime, settings; restricted to teacher/admin
Reason for Update: Role-restricted endpoint for teachers and admins to create exams with questions and scheduling metadata.

Functions: GET /exams — role-based filtered list
Reason for Update: Returns exams filtered by role: teachers see their own exams; students see only active public exams.

Functions: GET /exams/:id — hides correctAnswer for student responses
Reason for Update: Destructures and strips correctAnswer from the response payload before returning to student clients.

Functions: PUT /exams/:id — teacher/admin update with ownership check
Reason for Update: Allows only the original creator or an admin to modify exam fields; rejects all other users.

Functions: DELETE /exams/:id — restricted to original author
Reason for Update: Only the exam creator can delete their exam to prevent unauthorised removal.

Functions: handleSubmit() — client-side schedule and question validation
Reason for Update: Validates that scheduled time is in the future and all questions have a correct answer before sending to API.

Functions: addQuestion() / removeQuestion() / updateQuestion() / addOption() / removeOption() / updateOption()
Reason for Update: Fine-grained state management functions for dynamically building the question list in the exam editor UI.

Functions: fetchExam() — loads exam for editing when :examId URL param is present
Reason for Update: Populates the editor form with existing exam data so teachers can edit rather than recreate.

Functions: updateSetting(key / value) — toggles webcam / fullscreen / shuffle / passing score / maxAttempts / auto-submit
Reason for Update: Maps UI checkboxes/inputs directly to the exam settings object.

Functions: createExam() / getExams() / getExam() / updateExam() / deleteExam() — axios wrappers to /exams endpoints
Reason for Update: Centralised HTTP service layer for all exam CRUD calls.

Functions: virtual(totalPoints) — sums questions[].points
Reason for Update: Derived Mongoose virtual that dynamically totals question point values without storing the sum.

Functions: pre(save) hook — updates updatedAt timestamp
Reason for Update: Sets updatedAt to now on every save to maintain an accurate audit trail.

Functions: Access control: allowedStudents: [ObjectId] / isPublic: Boolean / accessCode: String
Reason for Update: Schema supports three enrollment strategies: whitelist / public flag / access code.

Functions: Correct Answer input — MCQ uses <select> from options; other types use <input type=text>
Reason for Update: Enforces that every question has a correct answer at creation time. Foundation for server-side grading.

Functions: points: Number (default:1)
Reason for Update: Marks allocation implemented. Word limits and difficulty levels are still pending.

Functions: POST /submissions — case-insensitive trimmed string match grading + score calculation
Reason for Update: Grades student answers using case-insensitive whitespace-trimmed comparison and sums points for correct answers.

Functions: Answer comparison: isCorrect = answer.toLowerCase().trim() === correctAnswer.toLowerCase().trim()
Reason for Update: UI marks each answer correct or incorrect in real time by comparing against the stored answer key.

Functions: pre(save) hook — percentage = Math.round((score / maxScore) * 100) with null guard
Reason for Update: Calculates and stores percentage automatically on every save. Null guard prevents NaN when maxScore is undefined.

Functions: PUT /submissions/:id/review — teacher sets score / reviewNotes / isFlagged / flagReason
Reason for Update: Backend endpoint allowing teachers to override the automated score and flag suspicious submissions.

Functions: handleReviewSubmission(isFlagged) — teacher approve or flag workflow
Reason for Update: UI flow that lets the teacher approve or flag a submission and add optional reviewer notes.

Functions: reviewedBy: ObjectId (ref: User) / reviewedAt: Date / reviewNotes: String
Reason for Update: Schema fields that store the reviewer's identity and feedback for audit purposes.

Functions: After review: creates exam_graded notification to student
Reason for Update: Sends an in-app notification to the student when their submission has been reviewed and graded.

Functions: POST /proctoring/event → logProctoringEvent() → fetchDashboardData() / fetchActiveSessions() (30 s polling)
Reason for Update: Event pipeline that flows from event logging to dashboard refresh within the 30-second polling window.

Functions: Standard Node.js event loop; MongoDB via Mongoose connectDB()
Reason for Update: Single-threaded async event loop with Mongoose connection pooling handles concurrent student sessions.

Functions: captureAndUploadScreenshot() — JPEG quality 0.5 at 320×240 every 60 s
Reason for Update: Limits screenshot payload size to ~20 KB per capture to stay within bandwidth constraints.

Functions: performAutoSave() — setInterval 30 000 ms sending answers[] / tabSwitchCount / fullscreenExitCount. Server: POST /submissions/auto-save — updates submission, increments autoSaveCount, sets lastAutoSave
Reason for Update: Saves student progress every 30 seconds so work is not lost on disconnection or crash.

Functions: POST /submissions/start — returns existing in_progress submission if one exists
Reason for Update: Allows a student to resume an interrupted exam session without losing previous answers.

Functions: startWebcam() — on failure logs webcam_disabled high-severity event but does NOT terminate the exam
Reason for Update: Camera failure is logged as a high-severity proctoring event but the exam continues so students are not unfairly locked out.

Functions: Passwords managed entirely by Firebase — hashed internally using scrypt-based algorithm
Reason for Update: All password hashing is delegated to Firebase; no passwords are stored in MongoDB.

Functions: Backend: POST /exams → 403 if not teacher/admin. PUT /submissions/:id/review → 403 if not teacher/admin. POST /submissions/start → 403 if not student. Frontend: RoleRoute wraps /create-exam (teacher/admin) and /take-exam (student).
Reason for Update: End-to-end RBAC enforced on both backend routes and frontend navigation guards.

Functions: requireAdmin middleware — returns 403 if user.role !== admin — applied to all /admin/* routes
Reason for Update: Dedicated middleware ensuring all admin routes are inaccessible to non-admin users.

Functions: GET /exams/:id — destructures out correctAnswer before returning to students
Reason for Update: Strips correctAnswer from the API response payload for student requests to prevent answer leakage.

Functions: Axios over HTTP (http://localhost:5000/api). HTTPS/TLS configured at reverse proxy level (nginx, Cloudflare, or Vercel).
Reason for Update: TLS is handled at the infrastructure level and not inside the application code.

Functions: Tier 1 — apiLimiter: 15 min window / 100 req max — global. Tier 2 — authLimiter: 15 min window / 5 req max — on POST /auth/register. Tier 3 — submissionLimiter: 1 hour window / 10 req max — on POST /submissions.
Reason for Update: Three-tier rate limiting protects API endpoints from brute-force and submission-flooding attacks.

Functions: calculateLightingLevel()
Reason for Update: Averages R+G+B pixel values from canvas ImageData to measure ambient lighting level in real time.

Functions: calculateFaceDistance()
Reason for Update: Computes Math.sqrt(boundingBox.width * boundingBox.height) as a proxy for face distance from the camera.

Functions: buildCalibrationThresholds()
Reason for Update: Generates acceptable distance bounds (baseline ± 20%) and lighting bounds (baseline ± 30) from calibration baseline.

Functions: POST /auth/login-failure — increments failedLoginAttempts; locks account (accountLockedUntil = now + 15 min) after 5 failures; alerts admins via Notification if ≥ 10 failures in 1 hour
Reason for Update: Records every failed login attempt and locks the account for 15 minutes after 5 consecutive failures to block brute-force attacks.

Functions: POST /auth/login-success — resets failedLoginAttempts to 0, clears accountLockedUntil, records LoginAttempt with success=true
Reason for Update: Resets all failure counters on successful login so the user is not incorrectly locked after a genuine login.

Functions: POST /auth/login-status — returns locked status + remainingMs for an email address
Reason for Update: Allows the frontend to check lock status before attempting login and display a countdown to the user.

Functions: POST /auth/check-registration — returns attempt count and lock status for an email address
Reason for Update: Allows the frontend to show remaining registration attempts before the 24-hour window locks the email.

Functions: POST /auth/2fa/enable — generates 40-char hex TOTP secret via crypto.randomBytes(20). POST /auth/2fa/verify — activates 2FA after correct code. POST /auth/2fa/validate — validates code during login. POST /auth/2fa/disable — disables 2FA after re-verification. GET /auth/2fa/status — returns enabled flag
Reason for Update: Full 2FA lifecycle using HMAC-SHA1 TOTP codes. Frontend uses examService 2FA wrapper calls.

Functions: enable2FA() / verify2FA() / validate2FA() / disable2FA() / get2FAStatus() — axios wrappers to /auth/2fa/* endpoints
Reason for Update: Centralised service functions for all 2FA operations from the frontend.

Functions: PUT /auth/profile — updates name / profileImage / referenceImage for the authenticated user
Reason for Update: Lets users update their display name and upload a profile or proctoring reference image.

Functions: GET /notifications — paginated list with unreadOnly filter. PUT /:id/read — marks one as read. PUT /read-all — marks all as read. DELETE /:id — deletes one notification. POST / — creates notification (teacher/admin/proctor). POST /broadcast — sends to multiple users by role or user ID list (admin only).
Reason for Update: Complete notification management endpoints used to surface proctoring alerts and exam events to users.

Functions: GET /admin/users — paginated with role/search/isActive filters. GET /admin/users/:id — user + stats. PUT /admin/users/:id — update role/isActive/name + notifies user. DELETE /admin/users/:id — cascades to submissions/exams/notifications/sessions.
Reason for Update: Full admin user management including cascading delete and role-change notifications.

Functions: GET /admin/exams — paginated with isActive/search filters. DELETE /admin/exams/:id — cascades to submissions and proctoring sessions.
Reason for Update: Allows admins to view and remove any exam along with all related data.

Functions: GET /admin/settings — returns system config (registration on/off, default role, session timeout, proctoring defaults). GET /admin/health — returns DB status, uptime, memory, and entity counts.
Reason for Update: Operational endpoints giving admins visibility into live system state and configuration.

Functions: POST /reports/generate — type: exam_analytics / student_performance / proctoring_summary / system_usage / flagged_submissions. Each aggregates data between startDate and endDate and saves a Report document.
Reason for Update: Five report types covering all major system dimensions. Persisted as Report documents for later retrieval.

Functions: GET /reports — list all reports (admin sees all; teacher sees own). GET /reports/:id — single report. DELETE /reports/:id — teachers can only delete their own.
Reason for Update: Standard CRUD for saved reports with ownership-based access control.

Functions: getMySubmissions() via GET /submissions/my-submissions — displays student's own submission history
Reason for Update: Role-guarded student view listing their own exam submissions with score and status.
